text:
Once upon a time in a small town nestled among rolling hills and serene lakes, lived a quirky cat named Bartholomew. Bartholomew was not your average cat. He had a passion for poetry, wore feathers as badges of honor, and believed he could communicate with the spirits of animals past.

One chilly spring day, while Bartholomew was curled up in his favorite antique bookshelf, he stumbled upon a dusty tome. Inside was the complete works of William Blake. Ejected pages fluttered to life, speaking in dreamy whispers.

Bartholomew listened intently. He couldn’t remember sleeping since discovering these words. The prose spoke tales of unity and freedom, of the natural world singing the song of creation. The spirits of ancient birds seemed pleased with his translation efforts; they urged him to keep writing.

Thus began Bartholomew’s mission. Every day after supper, he’d sit by his desk, surrounded by feathers and half-written sonnets. He
